chosen_action.title: Escalate active hold churn on the third event using already-effective rerun repairs

why_this_action.summary: The active 2026-05-25 hold window still has no truthful do-now packet, a post-hold rerun is already scheduled for 2026-05-25T07:20:16, and the loop was still one step away from logging another pretend follow-through. The highest-leverage executable fix was to make the hold churn guard respect the third-strike rule and reuse already-effective prompt/re-entry repairs from the recent repair history instead of demanding they be re-created inside every new hold window.
shared_findings_used: distribution_lane_latest.json: current lane remained measurement_hold with short-window release at 2026-05-25T07:20:16
marketing_execution_board_latest.md: no truthful do-now handoff packet exists in the current review window
marketing_2026-05-24_234934_active_loop_prompt_repair.json: active-loop prompt repair is already in force
marketing_2026-05-24_235759_post_hold_reentry_contract_repair.json: post-hold re-entry contract repair is already in force
marketing_2026-05-25_023723_measurement_hold_follow_through.md: scheduled post-hold rerun already exists for the active hold

result.summary: Patched measurement-hold churn detection so the third hold-window event escalates immediately, and so global prompt/re-entry repairs remain effective across subsequent hold windows. Added regression coverage and verified the focused measurement-hold executor suite.
